summary: Tested ScreenSense Expo app end-to-end. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests pass including real Claude Sonnet 4.5 AI insight generation via Emergent LLM key. Frontend: All 4 tabs (Today/Apps/Insights/Profile) and category detail screen render and function correctly with mock data. ONE blocker: onboarding 'Grant access & continue' button does NOT navigate to dashboard on web preview because RN-Web's Alert.alert polyfill ignores the buttons array, so the 'Continue' onPress callback (which calls router.replace) never fires. Direct navigation to /(tabs) confirms all tabs work.

minor.issue: Backend logs showed one Emergent LLM budget-exceeded error ('Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.005, Max budget: 0.001') AFTER the initial successful AI call. The endpoint correctly falls back to heuristic-generated summary/highlights/recommendations and still returns 200, but downstream insights for today may be the heuristic fallback (clearly distinguishable by the boilerplate text). First-of-day call typically succeeds; repeated regenerations may exhaust budget. Consider caching for the day (already implemented via /insights/today cache) and surfacing fallback state in UI if needed.

ui_bugs.issue: Tapping 'Grant access & continue' on web shows Alert.alert which on React Native Web only renders window.alert() and ignores the buttons[].onPress. Result: user is permanently stuck on onboarding screen and can never reach the dashboard via the UI flow on web preview. This is the only blocker for the documented flow 'Grant access & continue button navigates to /(tabs) after dismissing alert'.

issues: Category filter chips row visually overlaps the '20 apps · 8h 41m' summary line below it (chips and count text occupy same vertical space). Minor cosmetic — add marginTop on the summary or marginBottom on the chips ScrollView.

react_native_issues.issue: Non-blocking warnings observed: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' and 'Invalid DOM property transform-origin. Did you mean transformOrigin'. Likely from a library (lucide-react-native / react-native-svg). Safe to ignore for now.

action_items: Fix onboarding navigation on web: in app/index.tsx handleGrant, detect Platform.OS === 'web' and either call router.replace('/(tabs)') directly (skipping Alert.alert) or use a custom in-app modal instead of Alert.alert so the Continue button actually fires onPress. Native (Android APK) flow is fine because Alert.alert renders a real native dialog there.
Optional: tighten Apps tab spacing so filter chips don't overlap the summary count line.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 553-line file — acceptable for now but consider splitting into routes/ models/ services/ when extending.
All testIDs present on key interactive elements (good).
AI insight has graceful try/except fallback to heuristic — robust.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (5/6 flows; onboarding nav broken on web)

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds last 7 days of mock usage on startup and on first /api/usage/today call. No additional seed required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend runs at EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL https://habit-dashboard-app-1.preview.emergentagent.com — all /api/* endpoints verified. To bypass the onboarding-on-web bug for UI testing of tabs, navigate directly to /(tabs), /(tabs)/apps, /(tabs)/insights, /(tabs)/profile, or /category/<id>. After main agent fixes onboarding, retest by clicking grant-permission-btn and asserting URL changes to /(tabs).
rca_of_issue: Bug: onboarding stuck on web. Reproduction: open https://habit-dashboard-app-1.preview.emergentagent.com → tap 'Grant access & continue' → nothing happens (or only the browser alert appears with an OK button, but no navigation). RCA: app/index.tsx calls Alert.alert('Preview Mode', '...', [{ text: 'Continue', onPress: () => router.replace('/(tabs)') }]). React Native Web's Alert polyfill maps to window.alert() which displays only the message and does NOT support multi-button onPress callbacks — so router.replace never executes. Mitigation: in handleGrant, branch on Platform.OS: if web → directly call router.replace('/(tabs)'); else → keep current Alert.alert. Alternatively replace Alert with an in-app Modal that works on both platforms.
